nodes.context: Outlaw motorcycle club involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes in Nova Scotia from 1984 to 2003.
Biker gang in Halifax that patched over to become the first Hells Angels chapter in Atlantic Canada in 1984.
Montreal-based organized crime group involved in drug trafficking and money lending.
Gang founded by Randy Mersereau after he left the Hells Angels in 1998.

nodes.original_text_name: Marriot brothers, Billy and Ricky, of Spryfield
nodes.context: Spryfield-based gang allied with the Hells Angels, involved in drug trade and murder.

edges.context: The Thirteenth Tribe patched over to become the first Hells Angels chapter in Atlantic Canada in 1984.
The Hells Angels Halifax chapter owed drug debts to the West End Gang, which were forgiven in exchange for payments to Yves Trudeau.
Randy Mersereau broke away from the Hells Angels in 1998 to found his own gang, leading to his murder by the Hells Angels in 1999.
The Marriot brothers were allied with the Hells Angels, involved in drug trade and murder for the club.
